join_order.notes: Phase 1: scan sorted pairs sequentially; pairs are sorted by l_orderkey so groups are contiguous; accumulate l_quantity[row_id] per group; emit orderkey to hot_keys if sum > 300. No hash table needed. Each thread handles a disjoint range of complete groups by advancing start/end to group boundaries.
O(1) PK hash probe per hot orderkey; ~3000 probes total
O(1) PK hash probe per qualifying order using o_custkey
Binary search into lineitem_orderkey_sorted for Phase 2 quantity aggregation per hot orderkey

scan.access_pattern: sequential for sorted index; random for l_quantity by row_id

data_structures.phase1_agg: none — sequential sort-group scan of lineitem_orderkey_sorted replaces hash table entirely
data_structures.phase1_hot_keys: per_thread vector<int32_t> merged into flat_array<int32_t> (expected ~3000 entries)

parallelism.phase1_partitioning: divide 60M sorted pairs into nthreads equal chunks; each thread advances its start index forward until pairs[start].key != pairs[start-1].key (first complete group boundary); advances end index similarly so no key spans two threads; threads process disjoint complete groups with zero coordination
parallelism.phase2_partitioning: hot_keys split into nthread chunks, each thread processes its chunk independently

indexes.purpose: Phase 1: sequential group scan (pairs sorted by l_orderkey → contiguous groups, no hash table needed); Phase 2: binary-search range scan for per-hot-key quantity aggregation
O(1) PK lookup of orders row by o_orderkey
O(1) PK lookup of customer row by c_custkey

optimization_notes: Root cause (Category B — Algorithm change): Phase 1 (dim_filter) consumed 11626ms of 12147ms total. The iter_0 plan used per-thread std::unordered_map<int32_t,double> with reserve(1<<20) per thread — this causes 64×8MB=512MB of bucket arrays far exceeding the 44MB L3 cache, ~16M heap-allocated linked-list nodes (one per row processed), a single-threaded merge into a 128MB global hash table, and 16M free() calls during swap(). The fix is to eliminate the hash table entirely by using the pre-built lineitem_orderkey_sorted index for Phase 1: since pairs are already sorted by l_orderkey, groups are contiguous and a sequential scan with a running sum replaces the hash aggregation. Each of the 64 threads advances its chunk boundaries to complete group edges (pairs[start].key != pairs[start-1].key), processes contiguous groups sequentially with random l_quantity[row_id] accesses, and emits hot keys to a thread-local vector — no hash table, no heap allocation, no merge step. Expected Phase 1 runtime: ~480MB sequential index read at 50GB/s + ~480MB random l_quantity reads at 10GB/s with 64 threads = ~55ms total, down from 11626ms. Phase 2 (point lookups for ~3000 hot keys) is unchanged.
